summary:
The provided information confirms that mast cells are a component of normal dental pulp tissue along with other cellular elements such as fibroblasts and odontoblasts. Their role becomes more active during inflammatory responses but does not imply absence from healthy tissues; thus, they exist even under non-inflammatory conditions.

Okay, I need to summarize Agent1’s reasoning about mast cells based strictly on what was presented — focusing solely on clinically significant facts related directly to the query "Mast cells are".

First sentence should establish where mast cells reside – specifically within normal pulp tissue per the source material. Second must clarify any misconceptions regarding their presence versus activation state—emphasizing that while increased function occurs during disease/inflammation, these cells aren't exclusive to diseased areas nor missing at rest.

Final summary:

Mast cells are part of the normal composition of dental pulp tissue, coexisting with fibers and odontogenic cells like fibroblasts and odontoblasts. Although their functional activity rises during inflammation, they remain present throughout all stages including health without being restricted to pathological settings.
